findings.title: AtomicHarnessDefinition can be semantically rewritten, rehashed, validated, and receipted without governed authority

reproduction_evidence.method: Compile the governed definition, replace semantic and authority fields plus one section's source_refs and basis, recompute the canonical definition identity, call the production validator, then create a receipt.
reproduction_evidence.cause: validate() checks many upstream identities but does not bind the listed semantic fields to the governed definition input, checks authority_identity only for non-blank text, and derives expected section IDs from the candidate itself without checking each governed source mapping. Target validation then validates self-consistency and hashes rather than revalidating those meanings against upstream authority.

findings.affected_invariant: Only governed semantic authority may define the Atomic Harness Definition; a receipt must attest to governed meaning, not merely a self-consistent hash.
findings.recommended_correction: In ST-07.04, independently reconstruct and compare every governed semantic field and every section source mapping from hash-pinned upstream inputs; verify the authority identity against the ratification/authorized actor; make target validation invoke that independent validation before emitting PASS. Add mutation tests for each governed semantic and section field.

findings.title: The governed full suite fails twice and ST-07.04 has source without its governed tests or completion evidence
findings.affected_invariant: The synthetic demonstration cannot be called complete while its governed regression suite is red and its new validator path has no Story acceptance, integrity, failure, replay/invalidation, or architecture tests.
findings.recommended_correction: Complete only the already governed ST-07.04 test and completion scope, update the five authorized source inventories, then run the full suite twice. Do not infer completion from source presence.

findings.title: A post-commit observation failure reports command failure after target validation is durably committed

reproduction_evidence.cause: The atomic repository commit occurs before success observations. The broad catch treats the later observation error as NOT_COMMITTED and attempts another failing observation.

findings.affected_invariant: A command must have one unambiguous outcome; observability failure must not turn a committed command into a caller-visible failure or falsely label it uncommitted.
findings.recommended_correction: Use a transactional outbox or store observation intents in the atomic commit. At minimum, distinguish post-commit telemetry failure from command failure, return the committed receipt, and expose typed degraded-observability evidence without emitting NOT_COMMITTED.

findings.title: InMemoryRunRepository optimistic concurrency uses an unlocked check-then-write and can accept two writers while losing one event

reproduction_evidence.method: A barrier was inserted between _validated_append's version check and append's stream assignment; two threads submitted different version-3 events against expected_version=2.

findings.affected_invariant: Optimistic concurrency must permit exactly one writer for a stream version; every accepted write must be durable and replayable.
findings.recommended_correction: Make compare-and-append a single synchronized operation. Use a per-run lock or an atomic persistent-store compare-and-swap, and add a real concurrent-writer regression test that requires one success and one ConcurrencyConflict.

findings.title: Run lifecycle commands persist events, checkpoints, receipts, and command records through separate non-atomic calls

reproduction_evidence.injection: InMemoryRunRepository.save_command_record raises RuntimeError('injected_command_record_storage_failure') after create_run appends events.

findings.affected_invariant: Authoritative state, the command result receipt, checkpoint state when applicable, and the idempotency record must commit or roll back together.
findings.recommended_correction: Replace append/add_checkpoint/save_command_record choreography with repository transaction methods that prevalidate and atomically commit the event stream, receipt-bearing command record, and checkpoint. Add failure injection at every boundary and assert zero partial state.

findings.title: ZIP candidate identity and ZIP member descriptors are read from separate archive states

reproduction_evidence.method: A controlled workspace swapped candidate.zip after _read_unchanged returned archive A but before _scan_zip reopened the path.
reproduction_evidence.meaning: The returned candidate hash identifies archive A while the accepted descriptors identify archive B.

findings.affected_invariant: A Source Lock must bind descriptors to the exact bytes whose candidate hash was verified; archive mutation cannot cross that boundary.
findings.recommended_correction: Parse the verified in-memory archive bytes, or open one immutable handle and hash plus inspect that same handle while checking stable file identity. Add a deterministic archive-swap test.

findings.title: Checkpoint identities are mutable in the in-memory repository

reproduction_evidence.method: Store a valid checkpoint, then call add_checkpoint with the same checkpoint_id and changed state_hash/input_hash.

findings.affected_invariant: Historical checkpoint identities should be immutable even when corrupt checkpoints can be detected and bypassed.
findings.recommended_correction: Apply same-or-absent identity enforcement to checkpoints and provide an explicit test-only corruption injection seam instead of using the production repository method as an overwrite primitive.

findings.title: Five repeated exact-source inventories turn legitimate additions into unrelated regression failures

reproduction_evidence.assessment: The import and layering assertions protect architecture; the duplicated closed filename lists are maintenance tripwires and caused all five current suite failures.

findings.affected_invariant: Architecture tests should fail on boundary violations, not require repetitive edits to several historical Stories for every governed internal module addition.
findings.recommended_correction: Centralize the governed source inventory in one architecture contract/manifest and have historical tests assert the stable import/layering rules. Keep one owner test for unexpected-file policy.

findings.title: Active configuration and generated evidence contain machine-specific absolute paths

reproduction_evidence.classification: Several other hits are explicitly historical discovery records, rejection evidence, or captured interpreter commands and do not corrupt current canonical output identities.

findings.affected_invariant: Active dependency configuration and reproducible generated evidence should relocate without editing machine paths.
findings.recommended_correction: Use repository-relative release coordinates and required CLI inputs or logical source IDs in generators. Keep absolute paths only in clearly historical, non-portable audit evidence and never in deterministic identity payloads.

findings.title: The full suite emits an unset pytest-asyncio loop-scope deprecation warning

reproduction_evidence.message: asyncio_default_fixture_loop_scope is unset and future pytest-asyncio behavior will change

findings.affected_invariant: Test behavior should remain explicit across dependency upgrades.
findings.recommended_correction: Pin the intended asyncio fixture loop scope in governed pytest configuration before upgrading pytest-asyncio.
